description_long: This is a large, ancient-style ceramic vase with a wide, open mouth and a rounded, tapering body. The exterior surface is adorned with intricate, incised patterns, including swirling motifs and horizontal bands near the rim. The coloration ranges from light tan to deep brown, with visible darkened areas suggesting age or exposure to fire. The rim is slightly uneven, and the body shows signs of wear, including chips and missing sections, especially on one side. The interior appears hollow and unfinished, consistent with traditional hand-crafted pottery. This vase would be a striking decorative piece in an urban park, plaza, or historical display.
description: A large, weathered ceramic vase with swirling incised patterns and a wide, open mouth, featuring earthy tones and visible wear.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full, rounded body with detailed incised patterns and a mostly intact rim.
description_view_1: Side view reveals significant chipping and missing sections, especially along the right edge, with patterns partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view highlights the most damaged area, with a large portion of the body missing and minimal visible decoration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays more of the swirling patterns and a slightly uneven rim, with moderate surface wear.
